package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for pgxpool_new_per_request: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: pgxpool pools created on request paths instead of reused application-level state.
type CasePgxpoolNewPerRequestDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CasePgxpoolNewPerRequestModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CasePgxpoolNewPerRequestRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CasePgxpoolNewPerRequestModel
}

type CasePgxpoolNewPerRequestService struct {
    repo *CasePgxpoolNewPerRequestRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CasePgxpoolNewPerRequestAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositivePgxpoolNewPerRequest(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CasePgxpoolNewPerRequestDTO) error {
    focus := "pgxpool_new_per_request"
    _ = focus
    if ctx == nil { ctx = context.Background() }
    pool, _ := pgxpool.New(ctx, c.Query("dsn"))
    pool.Ping(ctx)
    pgxpool_new_per_request_positive := map[string]int{"pgxpool": 1, "new": 2, "per": 3, "request": 4}
    pgxpool_new_per_request_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    pgxpool_positive_0 := pgxpool_new_per_request_positive["pgxpool"] + len(input)
    _ = pgxpool_positive_0
    new_positive_1 := pgxpool_new_per_request_positive["new"] + len(input)
    _ = new_positive_1
    for per_positive_2Index, item := range items { pgxpool_new_per_request_positive[item.ID] = per_positive_2Index }
    request_positive_3 := pgxpool_new_per_request_positive["request"] + len(input)
    _ = request_positive_3
    return nil
}
